question: [File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc to the /topics/:topicId/messages endpoint to reverse the canonical ascending sequence of topic messages, causing downstream consumers to process messages in reverse order and reconstruct an incorrect transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both sequencenumber=gt:X and sequencenumber=lt:Y where X >= Y to force getRangeFromFilters() to return null and trigger an early empty return, silently omitting all messages from the response and making the topic history appear empty to any consumer relying on this endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user submit duplicate sequencenumber= filters (e.g., sequencenumber=gt:100&sequencenumber=gt:200) to cause getRangeFromFilters() to compute a tighter lower bound than intended, effectively skipping valid earlier messages and presenting a truncated history to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user submit duplicate timestamp= filters (e.g., timestamp=lt:X&timestamp=lt:Y) to cause getRangeFromFilters() to narrow the timestamp window and exclude legitimate messages from the result set, reorganizing the visible history by omission?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sequencenumber=eq:0 filter (below the defaultLower of 1n) to force getRangeFromFilters() to return null via the value < range.lower check, causing extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest() to return an empty object and silently suppress all topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sequencenumber=eq:9223372036854775806 (MAX_LONG - 1) filter to pin both lower and upper of the range to that value, causing the lookup query to return only the single partition containing that sequence number and hiding all other messages in the history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit=1 query parameter combined with order=desc to trigger the rangeExpression path that expands the lower bound by limit-1=0, causing the actual_range CTE to select only the last partition and omit all earlier topic messages from the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a very large limit value combined with order=asc to trigger the rangeExpression that expands upper(range) by least($4, 9223372036854775807::bigint - upper(range)), potentially spanning multiple partitions and returning messages from unintended timestamp ranges, mixing history from different time windows?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter that produces a timestampRange with lower > upper (e.g., timestamp=gt:9999999999999999999) to force getRangeFromFilters() to return null and cause getTopicMessageTimestampRanges() to return an empty array, making the entire topic history invisible?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the pagination link by observing the next link (which embeds the last consensus_timestamp) and then replaying requests with a crafted timestamp=gt:X to skip a window of messages, causing a consumer iterating pages to miss messages and see a gap in the sequence history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=1 to cause the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint to be omitted (since queryHint is undefined when limit === 1), potentially causing the PostgreSQL planner to choose a different index and return messages in a different physical order than the canonical consensus_timestamp order?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pagination next link is built using the last message's consensus_timestamp (not sequence_number) to craft a scenario where two messages share the same consensus_timestamp, causing the pagination cursor to skip or duplicate one of them and corrupt the visible message sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both sequencenumber and timestamp filters simultaneously to cause the topicMessageLookup path to intersect both ranges, where a carefully chosen timestamp range excludes the correct partition for a given sequence number, causing the query to return no results even though the message exists?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusTimestamp that matches a transaction with result=22 and type=27 but whose entity_id in the transaction table differs from the actual topic_id in topic_message, causing getMessage() to return a message from a different topic and misrepresent the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid consensus timestamps by probing the /messages/:consensusTimestamp endpoint and observing 404 vs 200 responses to reconstruct the full timeline of CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE transactions (type=27, result=22) without any authentication, enabling history mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validateConsensusTimestampParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusTimestamp with exactly 9 decimal digits (e.g., 1234567890.123456789) that passes isValidTimestampParam() but when parsed by parseTimestampParam() produces a nanosecond value that matches a different message than intended, causing the wrong message to be returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sequenceNumber at the boundary of isPositiveLong() (e.g., 9223372036854775807) to cause getTopicMessageTimestampRange() to query with a seqNum that overflows or wraps in the PostgreSQL int8 cast, returning a timestamp range from a different sequence number and fetching the wrong message?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the conditional topicMessageLookup path (config.query.topicMessageLookup) by sending requests when the feature is enabled vs disabled to observe different query behaviors, where the disabled path (no timestamp range filter) returns a different message than the enabled path for the same topicId and sequenceNumber?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a seqNum that matches multiple rows in topic_message_lookup (due to overlapping sequence_number_range entries), causing the query to return rows[0].timestamp_range (the first result ordered by sequence_number_range) which may not be the correct partition, leading getMessage() to fetch the wrong message?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NotFoundError by supplying a valid topicId and sequenceNumber that exists in topic_message but not in topic_message_lookup (e.g., due to a migration gap), causing the API to report a message as non-existent and creating a false gap in the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply sequencenumber=ne:X to trigger the InvalidArgumentError throw for the ne operator, causing an unhandled error response that differs from a normal 404, potentially leaking information about which sequence numbers exist vs do not exist in the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an unknown filter key (not in acceptedTopicsParameters) that is silently ignored by the switch default break, causing the filter to be dropped without error and the query to return a broader result set than the user intended, potentially exposing messages outside the requested range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user supply encoding=utf-8 vs encoding=base64 to cause TopicMessageViewModel to encode the message field differently, and if a consumer uses the encoding to reconstruct message content for history verification, a crafted encoding parameter could cause the consumer to misinterpret the message payload?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply sequencenumber=gt:X with X equal to MAX_LONG - 1 (9223372036854775806) to cause getRangeFromFilters() to compute lower = X + 1 = MAX_LONG - 1 + 1 = MAX_LONG - 1 (due to BigInt arithmetic), and then the range lower > upper check returns null, silently hiding all messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply sequencenumber=gt:9223372036854775806 (MAX_LONG - 1) to cause value += 1n to produce 9223372036854775807n (MAX_LONG), which equals range.upper (MAX_LONG - 1n), making lower > upper and returning null, causing the entire topic history to be suppressed?
